title: Full-loop scheduling: Samir Desai (SWE)
preview:
From: Samir Desai <samir.desai@noviretail.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 11 Oct 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Full-loop availability for Redwood

Hi Camila —

Thanks again for the friendly chat yesterday. I'm excited to move forward with the full loop. My calendar windows over the next two business days (PST) are:

- Tue Oct 12: 09:00–11:30, 13:00–16:00
- Wed Oct 13: 10:00–12:00, 14:00–17:00

I prefer morning slots, but I can do an afternoon if needed. I’m on Pacific time.

Attached my resume in case you need to share.

Best,
Samir
[Attachment: Samir_Desai_Resume.pdf (PDF, 210KB)]


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samir Desai <samir.desai@noviretail.com>, HireOps <ops@hirebridge.com>
Date: Mon, 11 Oct 2027 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Full-loop availability for Redwood

Jonas/Laura/Marissa/Logan —

Samir (candidate) sent availability below. Proposed plan to keep it to a single day (Tue Oct 12) with a tight panel sequence and a short debrief slot at the end:

Proposed multi-slot agenda (PST):
- 09:00–09:35 Technical system design (Jonas)
- 09:45–10:20 Deep dive: backend reliability & scaling (Laura)
- 10:30–11:05 Coding exercise (live pair) (Logan)
- 11:10–11:40 Behavioral / leadership (Marissa)
- 11:40–12:00 Debrief (internal only) — debrief owner: Marissa (can be shorter if everyone sends quick notes)

Notes / constraints:
- Candidate prefers mornings; this keeps buffer windows for short overruns.
- We'll need a backup interviewer for the coding slot in case of overlap. Jonas, can you cover backup coding if Logan needs to hop?
- Please review the attached runsheet for panel questions and timing.
- Logistics: plan to use Zoom room A for the first two slots, then switch to Zoom B for the pair-programming session (shared editor link in runsheet).

Confirm yes/no or propose swap by EOD today so I can send invites to Samir.

Thanks —
Camila
[Attachment: full-loop-runsheet-20271011.xlsx (Excel, 34KB)]

To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>

Also note I have a hard stop at 12:30. If the pair session runs long we should trigger the contingency: reduce design to 25m and combine part of behavioral with debrief notes asynchronously.

Laura

title: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Julie Hart <julie.hart@acme-robotics.com>
Date: Fri, 09 Oct 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics

Team —

Bringing the weekly fleet planning item over for the Acme pipeline that Julie flagged. They’ve moved their pilot to a POC costing call and finance asked for a committed slot proposal backed by a sizing note; procurement wants an indicative monthly reserve commitment range so they can budget for Q4.

Quick summary of asks from Acme (see attached forecast sheet):
- Sept–Nov pilot burn: mostly p99 short jobs, peak concurrency 22 inference workers; model base is a 13B quantized variant.
- Expected Q4 initial commit window: 4–8 dedicated slots (A100-equivalent) for steady throughput + burst for spikes.
- SOC2 evidence + audit log cadence requested by procurement.

What I need from you for tomorrow’s sales-finops-ops sync (Mon 10/11):
1) An ops-ready committed slot proposal: recommended slot size (vGPU/A100-equivalent), conservative headroom factor, and fallback (burst) plan.
2) Finance-facing unit economics: expected $/slot-hour and projected monthly token spend for the commit ranges in the sheet.
3) Any blockers for private-reserved deployment (networking/KMS) that would push them to Dedicated rather than Private.

I attached Acme_pipeline_forecast_v2.xlsx with their weekly concurrency numbers and SOC2 checklist stub. Please add notes inline; I’ll consolidate into the pre-close packet.

Thanks —
Aditya
Aditya Rao
Head of Strategic Accounts, Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0172

Attachments: Acme_pipeline_forecast_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet), soc2_evidence_request.pdf (doc)


From: Julie Hart <julie.hart@acme-robotics.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Acme Robotics Procurement <procure@acme-robotics.com>
Date: Fri, 09 Oct 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics

Aditya / team —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from our side while you size:
- The 22 concurrent workers are a peak engineering-run figure; expected steady concurrency is 10–12 for most weekdays.
- We plan to roll the 13B model across ~6 internal services (web inference, batch inference, rerank) — latency SLOs vary: web inference needs <150ms p95; batch rerank is tolerant.
- Procurement asks: provide two commitment tiers (Conservative: 4 slots; Growth: 8 slots) and the monthly cancellation window and burst billing approach.

We can be flexible on a two-quarter upfront commit if you provide a predictable pricing band and clear burst policy. Also, we’ll need SOC2 evidence by the contract signature date (we can accept a statement of intent + timeline if final PDF isn’t ready).

Julie
Julie Hart
Director, ML Ops — Acme Robotics
julie.hart@acme-robotics.com


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Julie Hart <julie.hart@acme-robotics.com>
Date: Sat, 10 Oct 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics

Folks —

I pulled the attached forecast and ran a conservative sizing pass. High-level recommendation:
- Committed slot baseline: 4 A100-equivalent slots (each slot ~1x A100 40GB throughput profile). This covers steady concurrency (10–12) with a 1.5x headroom to absorb config variability.
- Growth tier: 8 slots if they commit to the two-quarter upfront; reduces $/slot-hour by ~18% in our model.
- Burst strategy: enable burst credits up to +50% (short-term) with per-token overage billing. Ops can implement automated scaling to move >90s requests to batch pool when web p95 spikes are detected.
- SLA/Fallback: recommend a soft canary for the first two weeks (pin model versions, enable KV prefix caching) and an automatic fallback to quantized-8bit variant if latency regressions >15% are observed.

Finance note for Ben: I estimated monthly compute spend at current pilot qps for the 4-slot baseline at ~ $72k/month (compute only) with the Growth tier at ~$118k/month before discounts. I left room for token-cost variance in the sheet.

Operational blockers: the customer asked for KMS integration for customer-managed keys — infra may need a short checklist on network peering and KMS proof-of-concept steps. Marissa, can you confirm network lead time for a VPC peering + KMS onboarding?

Laura
Laura Bennett
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

> On Fri, Oct 9, 2027 at 09:12 AM Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Team —
> > Bringing the weekly fleet planning item over for the Acme pipeline that Julie flagged...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Julie Hart <julie.hart@acme-robotics.com>
Date: Mon, 11 Oct 2027 17:55:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly GPU orchestration: pre-close sizing request for Acme Robotics

Thanks — solid notes. A few additions from FinOps:
- Pricing bands: conservative 4-slot commit = $68k/month compute estimate after standard commercial discount (I trimmed Laura’s buffer slightly), growth 8-slot = $102k/month. Final numbers will depend on token volume reconciliation but these are what I’ll present to procurement.
- Cancellation: standard 30-day notice for monthly reserved slots; for the two-quarter prepaid we can offer prorated credits if they convert to a different commitment on >=60 days notice.
- Burst billing: overage billed at on-demand per-token rate with a cap defined in the SLA (we typically cap at 2x on-demand for emergency bursts).

Marissa — if ops can confirm VPC peering + KMS onboarding in <=10 business days we can put the two-tier proposal in the pre-close packet and route to procurement Tuesday. Julie — can you confirm whether Acme prefers prepaid-2Q or monthly reserved to guide discount band?

I’ll assemble the deck for procurement with the two commitment tiers, breakouts, and an ops checklist.

Ben Carter
Director, Finance — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Attachments referenced: Acme_pipeline_forecast_v2.xlsx (updated with cost bands)

title: MAP: finalize staging environment and close runbook
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chang <olivia@medflow.co>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 01 Oct 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: initial checklist for staging env and next steps

Hi Olivia —

Thanks for the sync earlier. Per our mutual action plan, I’m lining up the items we need from your side to get the POC staging environment ready and stay on the target close cadence the week of Oct 11. High-level checklist below — please confirm owners and ETA for each item: 

1) Org setup: A service admin for your org (email) so we can create the demo org and invite read-only observers. Owner: MedFlow SID?

title: Encryption key retention & transport protocol — evidence request
preview:
From: Alice Morgan <alice.morgan@northbridge.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: security@northbridge.io
Date: Sun, 03 Oct 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Evidence request — retention & transport of tenant keys

Hi Ben,

We are moving into the final stages of our SIG assessment and need a concise evidence set from Redwood about tenant key retention and transport protocols for BYOK deployments. Specifically: 

- Confirmation of how long wrapped keys are retained in Redwood logs/metadata after rotation (retention windows and purging process).
- Transport controls when customers provide wrapped keys over transit (TLS params, in-flight key handling, ephemeral endpoints).
- Evidence that key rotation events result in automatic deletion of derived key material (sample logs, not raw keys).
- Any third-party HSM attestations you maintain for private deployments.

Can you provide: a short runbook or checklist we can include in the SIG evidence package, a CSV mapping of rotation events to retention behavior, and a statement of the transport protocol? A redacted sample rotation log would help (timestamps, rotation id, operation type) — we do not need secret key material.

We are hoping to close this week. If possible, can we schedule a 30-minute sync Monday morning PST to walk through the artifacts?

Thanks,
Alice Morgan
Director, Security Assurance
Northbridge Financial

[Attachment: northbridge-sig-questionnaire.pdf (pdf)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alice Morgan <alice.morgan@northbridge.io>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>; Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 03 Oct 2027 11:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request — retention & transport of tenant keys

Alice — thanks for the clear ask. I'll assemble the artifacts and loop in our compliance lead, Laura, for the rotation logs and retention statements. Vivek, can you confirm whether we can share a redacted sample rotation log and the HSM attestation summary for private deployments?

Tentative attachments we can produce:
- byok-transport-checklist.pdf (checklist of in-transit handling and TLS profiles)
- kms_rotation_matrix.csv (mapping of rotation->retention/purge lifecycle)
- redacted-rotationlog-20270930.csv (redacted example: rotation_id, timestamp, actor, operation)
- hsm-attestation-summary.pdf (for private deployments only; public-hosted customers use cloud KMS statements)

I'll propose a 30-minute slot Monday 09:30–10:00 PST. Does that work?

Best,
Ben
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference

[Attachment: byok-transport-checklist.pdf (pdf), kms_rotation_matrix.csv (csv)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Alice Morgan <alice.morgan@northbridge.io>
Date: Mon, 04 Oct 2027 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request — retention & transport of tenant keys

Hi team — quick clarifications before I attach artifacts:

1) Retention: We retain rotation metadata and wrapped-key references (no key material) in the control plane for 90 days by default. We support custom retention for Dedicated/Private customers — policy can be set to 30/90/365 days; after the window we purge associated metadata via an automated job and record purge events in the audit stream. I'll include the retention-policy excerpt and a purge job log sample.

2) Transport: We accept wrapped key material over TLS1.3 mutual-authenticated endpoints (mTLS) for BYOK onboarding. For hosted onboarding we provide a pre-signed temporally-scoped upload URL (HTTPS, HSTS enforced; TLS1.3 ciphers only). The checklist outlines ephemeral endpoints and client certificate rotation guidance.

3) Rotation logs: We can provide a redacted CSV with rotation_id, event_ts (UTC), actor_role (system/customer), rotation_type (scheduled/manual/failover), and result. We will redact any identifiers that might be sensitive.

4) HSM attestations: For Private deployments we maintain vendor HSM attestation summaries (FIPS/HSM model/firmware) — these are summary PDFs, not full vendor reports.

I'll attach: byok-transport-checklist.pdf, kms_rotation_matrix.csv, and redacted-rotationlog-20270930.csv. Vivek — can you add the HSM attestation summary to the DocSend link and give a one-line auth statement for the packet?

Laura Bennett
Compliance Manager — Redwood Inference

[Attachment: redacted-rotationlog-20270930.csv (csv)]

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alice Morgan <alice.morgan@northbridge.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>; Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 05 Oct 2027 15:34:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence request — retention & transport of tenant keys

Alice — we uploaded the HSM attestation summary and an executive statement to the DocSend link below. Executive statement (for packet): 

"Redwood Inference confirms that for on-premise/private deployments where customer-managed HSMs are used, we maintain an attestation summary containing vendor model, FIPS level, and firmware baseline. Redwood does not retain raw key material; wrapped key references and rotation metadata are retained per customer-configured retention policy and purged automatically after the retention window. Transport of wrapped key material occurs over mutually-authenticated TLS 1.3 endpoints or temporally-scoped HTTPS uploads; ephemeral endpoints are used for onboarding and are rotated every 24 hours.

For your SIG pack we have provided: 1) byok-transport-checklist.pdf; 2) kms_rotation_matrix.csv; 3) redacted-rotationlog-20270930.csv; 4) hsm-attestation-summary.pdf (private deployments)."

Proposed next step: 30-min call Monday 10/11 at 09:30 PST for live walkthrough. If that works I will send calendar invite and DocuSign link if you require a statement acknowledgement.

Link to pack: https://docsend.example.com/view/northbridge-evidence-pack

Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Product Security — Redwood Inference

[Attachment: hsm-attestation-summary.pdf (pdf)]

title: Acceptance & invoicing cadence for HealthSync — onboarding bump + invoice tranches
preview:
From: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@healthsync.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ruth Patel <ruth.patel@healthsync.com>
Date: 2027-10-02T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Acceptance & invoicing cadence for HealthSync — initial ask

Hi Avery —

Thanks for the walkthrough yesterday. Our procurement team asked for a clearer acceptance schedule and an option to split the initial annual PO into tranches so our CFO can smooth cash flow while we ramp usage. We also want a short-term capacity bump for the first 60 days (approx +30% committed GPU hours) to ensure benchmarks on our integration tests.

Can you confirm:
- Whether Redwood can accept a single PO split into three invoices (50/30/20) across 90 days?
- If we can provision the extra capacity immediately upon PO signature for the 60-day ramp?
- What paperwork you need from us (SOC2 copy, line of business attestation, billing contact)?

Attaching a draft PO (stamped internal).

Attachments:
- HealthSync_PO_draft.pdf (PDF, 122KB)

Appreciate a quick confirmation — Ruth (CFO) is on the thread.

Thanks,
Tom
Head of Ops, HealthSync



From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@healthsync.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-02T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Acceptance & invoicing cadence for HealthSync — onboarding bump + invoice tranches

Tom —

Appreciate the clear ask. Short answers up front: yes on tranches (we can accept a single PO and invoice in multiple tranches), and yes we can provision the capacity bump on signature with a brief canary period. Below is the recommended, low-friction path we use for a configuration like this.

Proposed plan (high level):
1) PO & invoices: Customer signs a single PO referencing deal HS-2027-Q4-UPSELL-1427. Redwood issues three invoices on milestone schedule: 50% at acceptance, 30% at day 30, 20% at day 90. We can add explicit language for invoice dates to match HealthSync's fiscal calendar.
2) Capacity bump: On DocuSign signature we provision the +30% reserved capacity to a new "onboarding" pool for 60 days. After 60 days the pool scales down automatically to the committed baseline unless you ask to continue.
3) Acceptance window: We propose a 14-day technical acceptance window with a two-step signoff: (a) integration smoke tests (internal), (b) production canary verification (ops). If acceptance passes, final acceptance invoice is released.
4) Evidence & compliance: Please attach SOC2 Type II report and the billing contact; security team will send a short attestation checklist for data residency and retention.

Attachments I added for ops/legal: redwood_onboarding-runbook.xlsx (contains milestones, gating criteria, support SLAs).

Next steps:
- I will have Legal draft the invoice schedule clause and add a DocuSign flow for the PO.
- Kimberly, can you slot in the invoice milestone language and an acceptance clause?
- Marissa, can you confirm on the tax/PO remittance name for the 50% invoice?
- Tom/Ruth, if quarterly invoicing is a hard constraint, let us know and we’ll adjust cadence.

If that works I’ll kick off the DocuSign envelope today and schedule a brief ops handoff.

Best,
Avery Johnson
Account Lead — Redwood Inference



From: Ruth Patel <ruth.patel@healthsync.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@healthsync.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-03T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Acceptance & invoicing cadence for HealthSync — invoicing preference

On Fri, Oct 2, 2027 at 11:03 AM Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com> wrote:
> Tom —
> 
> Appreciate the clear ask. Short answers up front: yes on tranches...

Avery / Tom —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. Our preference is to keep billing quarterly if possible (one invoice per quarter) to match internal reporting. For the first 90 days we can accept three equally spaced invoices if necessary, but quarterly is strongly preferred.

Also: our security team will need SOC2 Type II and the onboarding runbook (we need to confirm data retention windows for logs). Can you provide the attestation checklist you mentioned?

I can route the PO once the invoice cadence is clarified.

— Ruth
CFO, HealthSync



From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Ruth Patel <ruth.patel@healthsync.com>, Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@healthsync.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-05T15:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Acceptance & invoicing cadence for HealthSync — final operational plan

Ruth / Tom —

Proposed plan (high level):
1) PO & invoices: Customer signs a single PO referencing deal HS-2027-Q4-UPSELL-1427. Redwood issues three invoices on milestone schedule: 50% at acceptance, 30% at day 30, 20% at day 90. We can add explicit language for invoice dates to match HealthSync's fiscal calendar.
2) Capacity bump: On DocuSign signature we provision the +30% reserved capacity to a new "onboarding" pool for 60 days. After 60 days the pool scales down automatically to the committed baseline unless you ask to continue.
3) Acceptance window: We propose a 14-day technical acceptance window with a two-step signoff: (a) integration smoke tests (internal), (b) production canary verification (ops). If acceptance passes, final acceptance invoice is released.
4) Evidence & compliance: Please attach SOC2 Type II report and the billing contact; security team will send a short attestation checklist for data residency and retention.

Hi Sara —

Congrats on the uptick — I saw BrightPath crossed the 250k token threshold yesterday from the self-serve project. That’s a great signal you might benefit from a short walkthrough to review optimization levers (batching, prefix caching, and a quick quantization tradeoff we’ve seen help similar customers).

Would you be open to a 30‑minute call this week? I’m proposing two slots:
- Thu 10/07 10:00–10:30 PT
- Fri 10/08 14:00–14:30 PT

If neither works, feel free to pick a time on my calendar: https://calendly.com/redwood-demo/30min

- review your current cost/latency profile
- show quick runtime knobs you can flip today
- talk about Dedicated vs sticking with self‑serve if you expect sustained growth

Best,
Ben Carter | SDR (Mid‑Market)

title: Inference network corridor and SLO alignment — post-demo follow-up
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@finapps.com>, Rajesh Kumar <rajesh.k@example-finapps.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 4 Oct 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Inference network corridor and SLO alignment — post-demo follow-up

Hi Elena / Rajesh —

Great to meet you both on the demo earlier. As promised, this note captures the run-of-show items and the minimal network/perf evidence we need to qualify the private/VPC deployment for your streaming inference workload. High level summary and asks below.

Quick summary (goal):
- Target 95th pct tail latency <= 120ms for small prompt (up to 512 tokens) in steady-state streaming.
- Sustained throughput target: 400 concurrent streaming sessions with ~30 tokens/s average per session.
- Cold-start concurrency: support 75 cold starts/minute during burst windows without violating latency SLOs.

Immediate asks from FinApps (what we need to validate):
1) Network path diagram (ingress/egress NATs, any transit providers, VPN/peering endpoints) — prefer a simple CIDR + ASN list and egress IPs.
2) Baseline egress bandwidth and per-flow RTT measurements from your app tier to our proposed VPC endpoint (detailed ip/port below) during a 2-hour window.
3) Firewall and proxy behavior for long-lived streaming TCP connections (idle timeouts, keepalive policies).

Attached: a suggested network test plan and an SLO alignment matrix (redwood-inference-network-test-plan.xlsx, slo-alignment-matrix.pdf). The test plan includes iperf3 commands, REQ/RESP size profiles, and a lightweight synthetic streaming harness we can run from your staging app nodes.

Suggested timeline / next steps:
- You provide network diagram + egress CIDRs by EOD Wed (2027-10-06).
- We run a short 2-hour baseline test Thurs 2027-10-07 morning (08:00–10:00 PT) — Ben will coordinate.
- Follow-up sync (30m) to review results Thurs 2027-10-07 11:00 PT.

Ben and Karthik (cc'd) will own the measurement orchestration and will ask for ephemeral creds and an agent install on one staging host. If you have a preferred testing window other than the one proposed, please suggest.

Thanks — looking forward to validating the path and getting you onto a Private deployment prototype.

Cheers,
Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.inference
+1 (415) 555-0183

Attachment stubs: redwood-inference-network-test-plan.xlsx (spreadsheet), slo-alignment-matrix.pdf (pdf)

From: Elena Park <elena.park@finapps.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>, Rajesh Kumar <rajesh.k@example-finapps.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 4 Oct 2027 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Inference network corridor and SLO alignment — post-demo follow-up

Thanks Aditya —

Appreciate the clear checklist. Quick answers inline:
- We attached a high-level path diagram to our internal ticket (I pasted the Drive link below). Our egress IPs live behind an outbound NAT at 54.83.12.0/24; we use a transit provider (Tier2Transit) with ASN 64512 for cross-region traffic.
- We can run the baseline tests from our staging cluster (stg-app-*) but we have an HA proxy in front of the app nodes that will terminate long idle connections after 5 minutes; we can temporarily bump keepalives for the test.
- Morning Thu (10/7) is OK for a 08:00–10:00 PT window; Rajesh will coordinate a short maintenance window so we can adjust proxy timeouts.

Forwarding our internal note:

> Great to meet you both on the demo earlier. As promised, this note captures the run-of-show items and the minimal network/perf evidence we need to qualify the private/VPC deployment for your streaming inference workload. High level summary and asks below.
> 
> [snip — test plan and SLOs attached]

Thanks,
Elena
Network Engineering Manager | FinApps
elena.park@finapps.com

Drive link (internal): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB-network-tests-finapps

Attachment stubs: finapps-egress-cidr-and-path.png (png)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>, Elena Park <elena.park@finapps.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
Date: Thu, 7 Oct 2027 07:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Inference network corridor and SLO alignment — post-demo follow-up

Morning all —

Per plan: Ben here. We will run the baseline from our side and also ask you to run the staged client harness so we capture both directions. Quick measurement plan (ops checklist):
- Redwood agent (temporary) will be SSH-installed on stg-app-1 (we'll provide a tiny install script). No access to secrets, only networking probes and the streaming harness.
- iperf3: 5-minute runs at 10 parallel streams to establish bandwidth.
- RTT and per-packet jitter: pings and tcptraceroute from stg-app-* to the proposed VPC endpoint (34.210.77.55:443) at 1s cadence for 10 minutes pre-test, then every 10s during load.
- Streaming harness: simulate 400 concurrent sessions for 15 minutes with token emission profile matching the demo (30 tokens/s avg, 200–600 byte token payloads).
- Logs we'll collect: per-connection accept timestamp, per-message latency, kernel-level retransmits, and egress bytes by source IP.

Can you confirm the staging host fqdn(s) and if we should use a bastion IP? Also: please confirm if the proxy keepalive will be raised to >10 minutes during the test. If you can paste the NAT/egress IP list here we'll update the firewall allowlist.

I'll upload the raw measurement template to the Drive link and post results into the HubSpot deal record after the run.

-Ben
Ben Carter
Platform Performance Lead | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference

Attachment stubs: measurement-template.csv (csv)
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/FIN-2027-0732

We will raise the HAProxy keepalive to 12 minutes for the maintenance window. Egress NAT block: 54.83.12.0/24 (pat to 54.83.12.7).

I'll add the test window to our calendar and will ping you when the bastion creds are live (short-lived key). Looking forward to the run — if the initial results look good we'll propose an A/B rollout plan for private deployment next week.

-Rajesh

Subject: Re: EU onshore mirroring + APAC standby question

Ben — thanks for the call today. A few follow-ups from procurement/legal before we can take the renewal amendment to our board:

1) Can you confirm the model residency guarantee in the EU after cutover (data at rest + backups)? We need explicit language for our DPA.
2) Sequence/timing: you mentioned APAC warm-standby will be activated after a month of EU steady-state — is that a hard dependency or configurable?

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>

Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2027 11:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: EU onshore mirroring + APAC standby question

Emma — thanks, great questions. Draft answers below and I’ve attached the SOW + traffic mapping for your review (see attachment list). Short answers up front:

1) EU residency guarantee: We will store production data and backups in EU-only buckets for the primary workload (we include this as a DPA exhibit). The SOW has the exact carveouts for audit logs and keys; keys remain in customer-managed KMS if you select that option.

2) Activation sequencing: APAC warm-standby is configurable. Typical plan we propose: (a) 30 days post-cutover for validation, (b) optional earlier activation for proof-of-concept (cost applies). The SOW includes two timeline options and associated lead times for capacity.

3) Pricing: Standby is priced as a reserved standby SKU + hourly burst. In the attached SOW we show two price bands: 
- Warm-standby reserved: USD 45k/month (covers baseline GPU pool sized to X tokens/s)
- Burst: metered at $0.09/GPU-hour for capacity above baseline

Questions for you: do you need HSM/KMS integration on day one or can that be staged? That affects the legal appendix.

Attachments: 20271004-SOW-eu-onshore-apac-warmstandby.pdf, carebridge-traffic-mapping.csv

Next steps: if those numbers look right, I’ll prepare an amendment (short form) and a mutual action plan with dates for cutover, test window, and post-cutover validation.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference

From: Emma White <emma.white@carebridge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: legal@carebridge.com
Date: Wed, 29 Sep 2027 08:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: EU onshore mirroring + APAC standby question

Thanks Ben — the SOW is helpful. A couple of follow-ups from legal/procurement after a quick pass:

- Legal: please add explicit language about log retention and geo-replication for backups (we need backups to remain in the EU unless we explicitly failover). Also include a 30-day notice window for any change to residency routing.
- Procurement: the standby reserved price (45k/month) is acceptable but we need a calendar-year commitment cadence and an option to scale down after Q1 if utilization < 30%. Can that be added as an amendment?

Also, can you confirm whether the DR failover test blackout windows will require any SLA credits if we exercise failover during business hours? We want to understand the operational / credit implications for scheduled tests.

If you can send a two-paragraph summary that we can paste into our board memo (clear TL;DR on residency + cost), that would speed approvals.

Emma

--
Attachment: excerpt_legal_comments.docx (referenced)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Emma White <emma.white@carebridge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 04 Oct 2027 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: RE: EU onshore mirroring + APAC standby question — TL;DR + amendment plan

Emma — thanks, appreciate the quick turn. Below is a short TL;DR for your board memo, and then our internal notes / requested legal edits we will incorporate. I’ve looped in Marissa (CS lead) and Karthik (solutions/infra) to confirm the tech/legal asks.

TL;DR for board (two paragraphs):
Redwood will provide EU primary residency for CareBridge’s production workloads with backups and standard audit logs stored in EU-only buckets. During the renewal we will add a paid APAC warm-standby pool to be activated on a configurable schedule; failover remains an explicit customer action and backups will not be geo-replicated to APAC unless CareBridge authorizes failover. The standby SKU offers fast activation for traffic surges with a guaranteed warm capacity and a metered burst rate.

Amendment plan / responses to legal: 
- Log retention & backups: we will add an exhibit that restricts automatic replication of backups outside the EU and a 30-day notification requirement for any residency routing changes.
- HSM/KMS: If you require customer-managed keys in an EU HSM, we can include KMS integration in the day-one scope; otherwise we will stage KMS in the first quarter after cutover.
- SLA credits for scheduled DR tests: scheduled tests performed during business hours will not incur SLA credits if they are planned and notified 14 days in advance; unscheduled failovers will be evaluated per the SLA. We will include a defined test window and rollback procedure in the runbook.
- Pricing cadence: add calendar-year reserved term for standby with a mid-term utilization review (Q1) and a scale-down option if sustained utilization < 30% for 60 days (with 30-day notice).

Internal action items (for our side):
- Marissa: please prepare the short amendment doc with the exhibits (legal redlines).
- Karthik: confirm lead time for EU onshore mirror cutover and estimate warm-standby spin-up time in hours. Also note any incremental infra cost assumptions to justify the $0.09/GPU-hour burst figure.

Marissa/Karthik — can you reply with ETA for the amendment draft and the cutover runbook? Emma, I’ll aim to deliver the amendment draft and a signed MOU by EOD Thursday (2027-10-07) if legal comments are light.

Thanks,
Ben

--
Internal note: Marissa to own amendment draft; Karthik to own technical runbook.

Subject: Onboarding invite - link appears truncated when forwarded

Hi Ben,

We received the supplier portal invite this morning but when our AP rep forwards the invite to the team the activation link gets cut off. Attached is a screenshot showing the email as it appears in Outlook (line wrap / truncation). We tried copying the link out of the email but the trailing query string is missing, which results in an error page on acceptance.

Attached: screenshot-truncated-link.png and our onboarding checklist (procureco-onboarding-plan.xlsx).

Can you confirm whether the invite URL is being wrapped or encoded in a way that breaks when forwarded? We're stuck on the PO submission step until the vendor can accept.

Tasha Lin
Accounts Payable
ProcureCo

ProcureCo Accounts Payable <ap@procureco.com>
Attachment: screenshot-truncated-link.png (image/png)
Attachment: procureco-onboarding-plan.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: ProcureCo Accounts Payable <ap@procureco.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-10-04T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding invite - link appears truncated when forwarded

Hi Tasha — thanks for the screenshot and the checklist. Sorry for the friction here.

Short answer: we have seen cases where mail clients (Outlook in particular) insert soft line breaks or hide long query strings when messages are forwarded, and that can strip the token portion at the end of our one-time acceptance link.

Immediate workarounds you can try:
- Instead of forwarding the original message, open the invite and click "Copy link" (if present) and paste into a new message.
- Ask the recipient to right-click the activation button and choose "Copy link address"—that usually preserves the full URL.
- We can also generate a replacement invite for the vendor contact directly from our portal (short-lived). If you want that, confirm the vendor contact email for the vendor account and I'll reissue.

I'm looping in Laura (AE) to confirm we can reissue a fresh invite and coordinate with your AP team so PO submission can proceed.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0183

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, ProcureCo Accounts Payable <ap@procureco.com>
Date: 2027-10-04T13:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding invite - link appears truncated when forwarded

Hi all — dug into the logs for the token in question. Summary: the acceptance token was generated and delivered intact from our SMTP relay. The mail client (Outlook web client / desktop) appears to be wrapping the href when the message is forwarded, converting the long query string into a line-broken fragment. In our case the token portion (query param "t=") is sometimes pushed to a new line and Outlook's forwarded message view inserts a hard ellipsis in the visible text.

What we can do immediately:
1) I can issue a new invite for the vendor with a shorter one-time token (shorter query params) which reduces chance of wrapping — validation TTL stays the same.
2) As a safer long-term fix, we'll update the invite email template to put the acceptance URL into a single <a href> button that also prints the full link in a preformatted code block underneath (so copy/paste preserves full URL even when forwarded). We'll aim to ship the template change into Prod next week; I'll add it to SUP-5921.

Tasha — if you confirm the vendor record:
Vendor name: Novacraft Supply
Primary contact: elena@novacraft.com
we'll reissue now and I will monitor the acceptance attempt in real time. If Novacraft still hits the error please forward the acceptance failure page screenshot and I'll capture the request/response trace.

- Jordan
Principal Product Engineer | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com

Subject: Re: Onboarding invite - link appears truncated when forwarded

Hi all — dug into the logs for the token in question. Summary: the acceptance token was generated and delivered intact from our SMTP relay. The mail client (Outlook web client / desktop) appears to be wrapping the href when the message is forwarded, converting the long query string into a line-broken fragment. In our case the token portion (query param "t=") is sometimes pushed to a new line and Outlook's forwarded message view inserts a hard ellipsis in the visible text.

What we can do immediately:
1) I can issue a new invite for the vendor with a shorter one-time token (shorter query params) which reduces chance of wrapping — validation TTL stays the same.
2) As a safer long-term fix, we'll update the invite email template to put the acceptance URL into a single <a href> button that also prints the full link in a preformatted code block underneath (so copy/paste preserves full URL even when forwarded). We'll aim to ship the template change into Prod next week; I'll add it to SUP-5921.

Tasha — if you confirm the vendor record:
Vendor name: Novacraft Supply
Primary contact: elena@novacraft.com
we'll reissue now and I will monitor the acceptance attempt in real time. If Novacraft still hits the error please forward the acceptance failure page screenshot and I'll capture the request/response trace.

- Jordan

title: Re: Region allocation & Singapore pilot - LatticeAI
preview:
From: Anya Petrov <anya@latticeai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: LatticeAI Sales Ops <ops@latticeai.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Sep 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Region allocation & Singapore pilot - LatticeAI

Ben,

Quick note from our side — we're planning to launch the new conversational billing assistant in APAC next quarter (target beta: early Nov). Expected traffic pattern: rapid ramp to ~600 QPS peak (short bursts) with sustained baseline around 120 QPS. Latency needs are tight for customer-facing flows (<300ms p50).

We'd like Redwood to propose a region-specific dedicated pool (Singapore primary, Frankfurt fallback) with an initial pilot for Singapore. Key asks:
- Estimated dedicated node count (A100-equivalent) for pilot and steady state
- Suggested model variants and quantization profile to hit latency + cost targets
- SKU split recommendations (APAC vs EU vs US) tied to deal pricing model
- Draft SOW and estimated timeline to get pilot live (PO/contract tasks included)

We can share traffic slices and example prompts but wanted to surface this now so we can lock capacity in time.

Thanks — can we get a preliminary sizing and proposed SOW by EOW?

--
Anya Petrov
CTO, LatticeAI
anya@latticeai.com
+44 7911 123456


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Anya Petrov <anya@latticeai.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Sep 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Region allocation & Singapore pilot - LatticeAI

Anya — thanks for the heads up and congrats on the product milestone. This is the right time to start capacity lock conversations.

Quick outline of next steps from our side:
- Priyom: commercial lead (pricing / deal-level SKU commitments).
- Irene: capacity planner (runtime sizing, model variant recommendations).
- Hannah: APAC operations and procurement lift for Singapore colocations.

Irene/Hannah/Priyom — can you take the asks below and put together a preliminary sizing + SKU split (APAC primary, EU fallback, US cold standby) + an SOW draft we can share with Lattice by EOW? If you need traffic histograms or sample traces from Anya, I can get them immediately.

Anya — will schedule a 30-min sync tomorrow to walk through the traffic profile unless you prefer a written summary.

Thanks,

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com


From: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: Anya Petrov <anya@latticeai.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Sep 2027 09:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Region allocation & Singapore pilot - LatticeAI

Ben / team / Anya — pulled together a preliminary sizing doc based on the profile Anya shared in the call this morning (thanks for the traces). Summary below; attached are a demand projection and draft SOW for the pilot.

Quick summary / assumptions (baseline):
- Workload: conversational generation, ~15-40 tokens per response average; ~12% embedding calls for search fallback.
- Latency target: p50 < 300ms (APAC), p95 < 1.1s.
- Model: recommend medium-flops variant of open-model + Redwood-optimized kernels (8-bit with KV-cache) for best cost/latency tradeoff.

Pilot (Singapore primary):
- Recommended starting footprint: 8 x A100-equivalent nodes (sustained), with 4 hot burst nodes for peak coverage (total 12 nodes for pilot). Forecasted to handle ~450 QPS peak with batching tweaks.
- Config: continuous batching with max latency window 25ms; KV cache enabled for repeat prompts (estimated 18% token savings).
- Fallback: Frankfurt pool (6 nodes) serving EU traffic and acting as regional fallback; US cold standby (3 nodes) routed only on cross-region overflow.

Steady-state (post-pilot, month+1):
- Increase APAC sustained to 14 nodes and keep 6 burst nodes for headroom (20 nodes total) if ramp follows projected adoption curve.

Risk & notes:
- Procurement lead times for Singapore racks typically 6-8 weeks after PO; recommend provisional short-term burst quotas to hit launch date.
- If Lattice wants lower p95, we need to increase burst nodes or pick a larger model SKU.

Attachments included: latc_usage_projection_q4.xlsx, proposed-sku-split_v2.pdf, SOW_singapore_pilot_draft.docx (stubs).

If that aligns with Lattice's expectations we can convert pilot numbers to a provisional PO and finalize the SOW.

Irene Choi

Subject: Re: Region allocation & Singapore pilot - LatticeAI

Anya — thanks, Irene's plan looks solid and addresses the latency/cost tradeoffs we discussed. High level next steps:

1) Lattice to confirm acceptance of the pilot sizing (12-node pilot in SG, Frankfurt fallback 6 nodes) by Friday EOD so we can start the procurement path.

Pilot (Singapore primary):
- Recommended starting footprint: 8 x A100-equivalent nodes (sustained), with 4 hot burst nodes for peak coverage (total 12 nodes for pilot). Forecasted to handle ~450 QPS peak with batching tweaks.
- Config: continuous batching with max latency window 25ms; KV cache enabled for repeat prompts (estimated 18% token savings).
- Fallback: Frankfurt pool (6 nodes) serving EU traffic and acting as regional fallback; US cold standby (3 nodes) routed only on cross-region overflow.

Steady-state (post-pilot, month+1):
- Increase APAC sustained to 14 nodes and keep 6 burst nodes for headroom (20 nodes total) if ramp follows projected adoption curve.

Risk & notes:
- Procurement lead times for Singapore racks typically 6-8 weeks after PO; recommend provisional short-term burst quotas to hit launch date.
- If Lattice wants lower p95, we need to increase burst nodes or pick a larger model SKU.

title: Streaming sawtooth p99 regression — peak-night impact
preview:
From: Jin Park <jin.park@kestrelretail.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 19 Sep 2027 18:05:12 -0700
Subject: Streaming sawtooth p99 regression tonight — urgent

Camila / Redwood team,

We're seeing a pronounced p99 spike and a sawtooth pattern on our SSE chat traffic that started ~17:40 PT tonight. Symptoms: p95 is up ~30%, p99 up from ~600ms to ~2.2s, and a growing rate of 502/504 errors when connections recycle. Long-lived streams (5–10+ minute sessions) are timing out or reconnecting repeatedly. We also observed bursts of 429s on the /v1/streams endpoint.

Attached is a short trace and a small log excerpt (kestrel-sse-tail-sample.txt). Key log snippets show repeated \"kv cache miss -> rehydrate\" messages and fast oscillations in request queue depth. This is affecting our checkout chat widgets on the mobile app (high traffic right now).

Can you confirm whether this is a known regression or if something in your routing/ingress changed? Our SRE has PagerDuty open.

- Jin
Product Ops, Kestrel Retail

[attachment: kestrel-sse-tail-sample.txt (text)]

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Rohan Mehta <rohan.mehta@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jin Park <jin.park@kestrelretail.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Sep 2027 18:14:33 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Streaming sawtooth p99 regression tonight — urgent

Forwarding Kestrel's report — hitting them now but want an internal triage ASAP. Summary: sustained p99 tail increase with sawtooth/oscillation in metrics and increased 5xx/429s on SSE. Impacting long-lived sessions. Kestrel is in peak traffic window (mobile checkout).

Immediate asks:
- Marcus: can you correlate Kestrel's request IDs with ingress/routing spikes and share a quick metric snapshot?
- Rishi/Rohan: any deployments or autoscaler changes in the last 3 hours? Could this be batching or eviction behavior under high concurrency?
- Stephanie: please prepare a brief status note for the customer and draft an internal exec blurb if we need to escalate.

PagerDuty link: PagerDuty: PD-20270919-1
Status page link: https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/INC-20270919

I'm standing by on this.

— Camila
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

--- Original message attached ---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Rohan Mehta <rohan.mehta@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jin Park <jin.park@kestrelretail.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Sep 2027 18:22:50 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Streaming sawtooth p99 regression tonight — urgent

Camila / team,

Quick findings from the first 15 minutes of triage (attached: metrics_snapshot.png):
- I can reproduce the sawtooth in the ingress LB (5–8s oscillation). CPU on the streaming workers is spiking in waves.
- Continuous batching appears to be enlarging up to 30–40ms windows under bursty arrivals which creates brief queue buildups, then drains — results look like a sawtooth on p99.
- I also see repeated KV-cache rehydrates on model workers for Kestrel's tenant (likely due to cache eviction from high concurrently-serving contexts). That increases per-request latency heavily for those cache-miss windows.
- No routing config rollouts in the last 3 hours, but autoscaler has been aggressive on scale-to-zero boundaries for low-traffic pools and may be slow to respond to a sudden peak (we saw ~60s scaling lag on one pool).

Immediate mitigation suggestions (fast, reversible):
1) Increase min replicas for the streaming pool serving Kestrel to avoid cold evictions (hotfix to autoscaler policy).
2) Reduce batching window from 30ms -> 10ms for streams for this tenant to smooth queue spikes (config flag at the routing layer).
3) Temporarily pin prefix cache for Kestrel (cache pinning) to prevent rehydrate storms.
4) As a last resort, route Kestrel to a smaller-quantized model variant for the next 30–60 minutes to lower per-token compute.

I'll prepare a change set for (1) + (2) and a quick rollback plan. ETA for first change to be live ~12 minutes after approval.

— Marcus
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference

[attachment: metrics_snapshot.png (image)]

On Sun, Sep 19, 2027 at 6:05 PM Jin Park <jin.park@kestrelretail.com> wrote:
> Camila / Redwood team,
> ... (original report)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rohan Mehta <rohan.mehta@redwood.ai>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jin Park <jin.park@kestrelretail.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Sep 2027 18:35:10 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Streaming sawtooth p99 regression tonight — urgent

Thanks Marcus — that aligns with what I suspected. Proposed immediate plan:
- Approve hotfixes 1 and 2 from Marcus (increase min replicas, reduce batching window). Marcus, go ahead and implement and monitor. If the autoscaler change needs an infra rollback, Rohan please stand by.
- I will stage the routing fallback to the smaller-quantized model and keep it disabled unless we see no improvement after the first 15 minutes.
- Rohan: please monitor GPU RM and KV cache hit rate; push a quick alert if cache hit drops > 10% vs baseline.

We should avoid a full rollout to other tenants. Keep changes scoped to Kestrel's tenant-specific routing policy to reduce blast radius.

If we see no improvement by 19:10 PT, escalate to execs and prepare an incident bridge.

— Rishi
Engineering Lead, Serving Runtime

title: Weekly cross-functional pulse: open asks, exec clarifications, and escalation log
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Weekly cross-functional pulse: open asks, exec clarifications, and escalation log

Team — following our weekly sync this morning, I want to capture the open asks (particularly the exec-level clarifications) and surface one item that needs an escalation path for QBR prep. I attached the metrics slate for the QBR as reference. Please read and action the items below; update the Prod-Exceptions tracker when you make changes.

High-level asks (action owners + due):
- Exec ask 1: Dedicated capacity pinning — can Product confirm whether we can guarantee model pinning for Reserved GPU pools for Q4? (Owner: Karthik) — due: 2027-09-16 EOD
- Exec ask 2: SLA language for marketplace customers (Acme) — legal needs the wording we can commit to for Dedicated nodes. Marketing and Sales want the short blurb for the QBR deck. (Owner: Marissa) — due: 2027-09-17 noon PT
- Exception log: We have 12 outstanding model-quality regressions tied to recent quantization profiles. Need prioritization for rollbacks vs. mitigations. (Owner: Vivek) — due: 2027-09-18

Notes / dependencies:
- Acme procurement is targeting signature next week; Sarah (Acme) asked for firm SLA and a description of fallback behavior if Dedicated capacity is constrained. She was on the call — I've CC'd her below.
- Drive: QBR-Metrics-Slate.xlsx attached (also in Drive link below).
- Please update the Prod-Exceptions-Tracker CSV with status and immediate mitigation steps.

Requested reply format: one-line updates per ask (Done / In progress / Blocked) with ETA. If you need to escalate to execs, reply with the impact statement.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Head of GTM Ops (acting)
Redwood Inference

Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 10:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly cross-functional pulse: open asks, exec clarifications, and escalation log

Ava — quick update on pinning:
- In progress. Engineering confirmed we can support model pinning for Reserved pools for the vast majority of cases, but there are two caveats: (1) extreme model sizes (>120B) may require a fallback to a compatible variant when hardware constraints trigger oversubscription, and (2) certain quantized builds (int4 experimental) do not yet have identical output parity guarantees.
- Action: I'll produce a short tech blurb for QBR (1-2 bullets) and a separate appendix for when fallbacks occur. ETA: 2027-09-16 EOD.

Blocking: Need Product to confirm whether we allow customer-controlled fallback toggles (i.e., allow Acme to opt-in to cheaper variants that may have slightly different output). That affects SLA language.

Karthik
Director, Product
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@acme.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2027 08:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly cross-functional pulse: open asks, exec clarifications, and escalation log

Thanks — looped in Sarah for a procurement perspective and Dan (CFO) on copy.

SLA language draft (working):
- "Redwood Inference will provide 99.5% availability for Reserved Dedicated GPU pools during the contractual window. In the rare event of resource constraints, Redwood will: (a) route to compatible pinned model variants where available, or (b) notify the customer and execute pre-agreed fallback behavior outlined in Appendix A."

Notes: Legal wants us to avoid committing to absolute identical outputs post-fallback — we should phrase as "compatible model variants" plus a remediation credit framework. I'll get a tidy paragraph for the QBR deck.

Attachment: Prod-Exceptions-Tracker.csv (CSV, 42KB) — I updated the rows for the 12 regressions and added columns for mitigation priority (High/Med/Low) and rollback candidate.

Ask for Acme: Sarah — can you confirm whether Acme is comfortable with "compatible model variants" plus a 10% credit if output delta threshold exceeds the agreed tolerance? If not, pls propose language.

Marissa
Director, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

Procurement: signature ETA is 2027-09-22. Waiting on final CFO approval. DocuSign link to follow.

To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>

title: Post-demo: SDK auth + JSON failover strategy (next steps)
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Harris <alex.harris@crestwave.ai>, Sasha Lim <sasha.lim@crestwave.ai>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: SDK auth + JSON failover strategy (next steps)

Hi Alex / Sasha — thanks again for the demo today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum: 

1) Scope / outcomes we agreed
- 2-week POC: embeddings + re-ranker + structured output for search result normalization
- Success criteria: F1 > 0.78 on your labeled 5k queries; <200ms median gen latency for short responses

2) Integration items we need to action (we can split between infra and app):
- SDK/setup: add redwood-js v2.1.0 into backend service; we recommend server-side token flow (short-lived key + rotation)
- Auth: service-to-service JWT + per-route token scope for model inference (discussed audit logs requirement)
- Routing: prefer region us-west for latency; fallback policy to cheaper variant when load > 85%
- Structured output: agreed JSON envelope for results + explicit schema for \"hits\" array
- Validation + retry: schema validation at the SDK layer, automated constrained decoding with token cutoff + deterministic retry policy for malformed JSON

3) Ask from CrestWave (items you promised to provide)
- Sample dataset: 5k anonymized query/ground-truth pairs (can be a csv)
- List of production endpoints and expected QPS for sizing

Attachments: redwood-integration-checklist.xlsx; auth-routing-diagram.png; schema-fallbacks.md

Next step: can we confirm internal owners + agree a kickoff on Monday 2027-09-20? I suggest Dev/Marcus + your backend lead.

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Sr. Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[a] redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0112

--
This email is from Redwood Inference. For more on our POC templates and SDKs see https://docs.redwood.ai/poctemplates


From: Alex Harris <alex.harris@crestwave.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sasha Lim <sasha.lim@crestwave.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: SDK auth + JSON failover strategy (next steps)

Aisha — great summary, thanks. Quick answers inline: 

- Dataset: we'll deliver a 5k CSV (anonymized) by Friday 2027-09-17. I'll include column defs.
- QPS: initial target is 8–12 QPS with spikes to 35 QPS during peak ETL windows.
- Security: we need request-level audit logs + SOC-style evidence for the POC. Can you confirm what log retention you support for Private/Dedicated?

On the JSON thing — Sasha and I want the POC to be tolerant to occasional schema drift. Can you map out how Redwood recommends handling partial/malformed outputs at the service level (reject vs. best-effort) and how retries interact with idempotency?

Suggested kickoff is fine — Monday 2027-09-20 works for us (Sasha, Alex, your engineering lead).

Best,
Alex Harris
Chief Product Officer | CrestWave AI
alex.harris@crestwave.ai | +1 212-555-0190

[attachment: crestwave-poc-reqs.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)]


From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Harris <alex.harris@crestwave.ai>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2027 11:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: SDK auth + JSON failover strategy (next steps)

Alex / team — copying the tech details you asked for so we can scope the POC precisely. High-level approach we've used successfully elsewhere: 

1) Auth & audit logs
- For POC: server-side short-lived API key (30m) exchanged via an internal auth service. Production: recommend Dedicated or Private with KMS-backed signing.
- Audit: per-request trace id + event stream; retention for Dedicated is configurable (30/90/365 days). For Private we can retain per your KMS policy — we support exportable log bundles for SOC evidence.

2) SDK/setup and region routing
- Use redwood-js v2.1.0 (node >=16). Example usage to call embedding + reranker endpoints: 
  const Redwood = require('@redwood/inference');
  const client = new Redwood({ apiKey: process.env.REDWOOD_KEY, region: 'us-west' });

  // embeddings
  const emb = await client.embeddings.create({ model: 'rw-embed-2', input: query });

- Routing: implement primary route to us-west and a lower-cost fallback model (rw-compact-1) that is compatible for re-ranking when primary is saturated. SDK supports routePolicy: 'latency' | 'cost' in the client config.

3) Structured output reliability (JSON schema + constrained decoding)
- Envelope: { "request_id": "", "hits": [ {"id":"","score":0.0,"metadata":{} } ], "model_version": "" }
- Schema validation: we recommend validating the envelope at the SDK boundary using ajv (json-schema). If validation fails, two options controlled by a flag: 
  a) fail-fast: return error to caller (useful for strict pipelines)
  b) best-effort: attempt automatic repairs:
     - run a constrained-decode pass (temperature=0.0, max_new_tokens small) to coerce output into schema
     - if still invalid, fallback to cached response or try the compact model (rw-compact-1)
- Retry policy: idempotency key = request_id. Retries only for transient server errors (5xx) or for deterministic repair attempts; do NOT automatically retry on 4xx validation failures unless caller explicitly opts-in.

4) Embeddings & reranker notes
- Use batch embeddings with size 64 for latency/cost balance; store vector ids and a short metadata blob.
- Reranker should accept candidate ids and return normalized scores + optional structured corrections. We recommend an integration test that verifies stable reranker ordering on your 500 sample queries before full indexing.

5) Implementation checklist (what we'll deliver for POC)
- Sandbox project + API key (expires 30d)
- Sample SDK integration PR with schema validation + retry hooks
- Runbook: how to interpret failed JSON + mitigation steps (repair -> fallback -> manual review)

Snippet: sample constrained-decode call (pseudo):
  // server-side repair attempt
  const repaired = await client.completions.create({
    model: 'rw-general-1',
    prompt: promptForSchema(envelopeSchema),
    temperature: 0.0,
    max_new_tokens: 64,
    stop: ['\
\
']
  });

I'll prepare the SDK PR and a short demo script for the kickoff. If you can share the sample CSV and your expected QPS windows, we can size Dedicated.

Thanks,
Dev Patel
Senior Software Engineer, Platform Integrations | Redwood Inference
[attachment: sample-sdk-pr.diff (text/x-diff)]

title: Billing schedule options & short-pay incentive for onboarding
preview:
From: Rebecca Lin <rebecca.lin@mosaicdata.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2027 09:14:22 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding — billing schedule options

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick intro call this morning. Mosaic is excited to get the integration started. To speed finance onboarding, AP asked I share our preferred billing cadence and a couple of scenarios we can accept depending on Redwood's setup.

Quick background: most of our SaaS partners run Net30 or Net45, but we can be flexible if a split or milestone billing is preferred for the initial integration phase. Attached are: the completed vendor onboarding form, our W9, and a sample invoice (PDFs).

Options we can accept:
- Standard Net30 (preferred)
- Net45 (if Redwood has a standard onboarding window)
- Split: 50% milestone on activation / 50% on 30 days after go-live

One more thing — we can offer a short-pay (discount) for faster payment: 1.5% if paid within 10 days. If Redwood can commit to that for the first 90 days, we can expedite invoice processing on our side.

Let me know what works and any vendor docs you need from us.

Best,
Rebecca Lin
Head of Finance Ops
Mosaic Data
[attachment: mosaic_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf] [attachment: mosaic_w9.pdf] [attachment: sample-invoice-September.pdf]


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rebecca Lin <rebecca.lin@mosaicdata.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2027 11:02:07 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding — billing schedule options

Thanks Rebecca — and great to meet you on the call. Appreciate the attachments.

Short answers up front: Redwood's default for new vendor activations is Net45, but there are a few levers we can use depending on the account and contract:
- Net30 or Net45 are both doable as part of onboarding.
- We can set up split milestone invoicing for the first two invoices while integration is in progress.
- We have an early-payment incentive program that Procurement (Priyom) can opt the vendor into — typical discounts range from 1%–2% for a 10-day pay window.

Priyom — can you confirm whether we should: (A) proceed with Net45 standard, (B) allow Net30 for Mosaic once vendor is validated, or (C) enroll Mosaic in the short-pay program at 1.5% for pay within 10 days? If C, we should capture the discount in the PO terms and note it on the invoice metadata so AP knows to process accordingly.

Rebecca — if you could confirm which option you prefer, we can finalize PO terms and route to Redwood AP for activation. Also flagging Marissa from CS for the milestone schedule so we can align billing to deliverables.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales

title: Post-demo: Crescent AI integration recap & networking open items
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@crescent.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: Crescent AI integration recap & networking open items

Hi Samira / Ethan —

Thanks again for your time today. Quick recap and the open items we promised to follow up on after the demo: 

1) Integration surface (what we demonstrated)
- SDK: JavaScript + Python quickstart, server-side token handling. We showed structured output routing to your toolchain (webhook -> worker) and the basic embeddings flow for semantic search.
- Auth options: hosted API key for quick POC; VPC endpoint + mTLS for production (we discussed both).
- Routing: model pinning for latency-sensitive endpoints and automatic fallback to open quantized variant.

2) Networking / private connectivity (technical asks from call)
- VPC endpoint: we support AWS PrivateLink and a VPC endpoint option for Dedicated/Private. If you want PrivateControlPlane (VPC + peering), we’ll provide endpoint manifest and required IAM roles.
- IP allowlist: we can provide a stable set of egress IP ranges for your firewall rules; need your target subnets and any proxy in front of Redwood.
- Egress & DNS: DNS egress to our resolver or use your own with split-horizon — note that we recommend explicit DNS for structured-output webhook destinations.
- mTLS: we support client-side mTLS for the endpoint; mutual certs are rotated every 90 days by default. We can accept your CA bundle or run cert rotation via ACM (if you prefer).

3) Embeddings & reranker pipeline notes
- We recommend a two-stage approach: 1) embeddings + vector store (managed or your choice) and 2) a lightweight reranker on a smaller Redwood model for precision. We can supply a starter rerank prompt set and eval harness.

4) Structured output / tool calling
- Outbound tool calls (webhooks): signed requests with HMAC + timestamp are supported. For internal tool invocation over private network, we recommend mTLS + IP allowlist.

Next steps / action items
- Crescent: share target VPC subnet ranges and whether you prefer PrivateLink or peering (Samira).
- Crescent: indicate whether certs will be customer-managed or Redwood-managed for mTLS.
- Redwood: provide network diagram (attached), integration checklist (attached), and SOW draft for the POC (attached).
- Schedule: propose a 60-minute tech kickoff the week of Sep 20 — please share 2 windows that work for you.

I’m copying Ben (solutions) and Laura (infra lead) to pick up the deeper tech threads.

Thanks,

— Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: crescent-network-diagram.pdf, redwood-integration-checklist.xlsx, sow-draft-v1.docx



From: Samira Khan <samira@crescent.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ethan Cole <ethan@crescent.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Crescent AI integration recap & networking open items

Thanks, Ava — great session. A few clarifying questions before we lock the kickoff: 

1) mTLS certs: Can Redwood accept our internal CA so we keep rotation in-house? Or is the Redwood-managed path strongly preferred? We have strict audit controls that prefer customer-managed keys.

2) IP ranges: We need the full list of egress IPs (not just endpoints) for our security team to complete the allowlist. Can you provide IPv4 and IPv6 ranges if available?

3) Embeddings throughput: for our initial POC we expect ~2000 queries/day with 2k-length context. Any guidance on cost & recommended model/quantization?

4) Tool calling: our webhook consumer sits behind an internal load balancer that verifies client cert CN — does Redwood allow us to enforce CN validation on incoming calls?

Proposed kickoff windows: Sep 21 10:00–11:00 PT or Sep 22 14:00–15:00 PT.

Samira
Head of Platform Engineering | Crescent AI
samira@crescent.ai | +1 (650) 555-2299

On Wed, Sep 15, 2027 at 3:05 PM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (post-demo notes)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@crescent.ai>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2027 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Crescent AI integration recap & networking open items

Hi Samira — quick answers from infra/solutions side: 

1) Customer-managed mTLS certs: yes, we can accept your CA bundle. We'll add a short integration doc for your PKI team that covers CSR format, allowed CN/SAN patterns, and HTTP client cert validation. We also support Redwood-managed rotation if you want us to operate cert lifecycle (easier for POC).

2) Egress IPs: Attached is the current published egress set for Redwood hosted endpoints (v4). We will provide a specific subset for PrivateLink/Dedicated once we scope the Dedicated pool. We do not publish IPv6 to all customers yet; if required we can provision an IPv6-enabled endpoint for production.

3) Embeddings cost & model: For your volume (2k/day), start with ada-like open embeddings for cost efficiency. If you need vector similarity quality improvement, upgrade to a mid-tier model or add a reranker. I added estimated token & cost numbers in the checklist attachment.

4) CN validation on tool calls: yes — our webhook client supports validating the peer cert CN/SAN on your load balancer. If you require an additional HMAC header we can sign requests as well.

Action: I'll prepare the cert integration doc and an IP manifest for the POC environment by EOD Sep 17.

- Ben
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

> On Thu, Sep 16, 2027 at 09:12 AM Samira Khan <samira@crescent.ai> wrote:
> > Questions on mTLS / IPs / throughput

Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 17 Sep 2027 09:23:00 -0700

- Ben will walk PKI integration (CSR, CA bundle acceptance, CN/SAN expectations) and show a short mTLS demo.
- Laura will cover network patterns: PrivateLink vs peering tradeoffs, expected egress IPs for the POC, and DNS recommendations.
- I’ll run through the integrations checklist and SOW milestones for the POC.

Deliverables before kickoff (we will send these by EOD Sep 17):
- Ben: cert integration doc + sample CSR + Redwood trusted CA acceptance checklist.
- Ben: POC egress IP manifest (v4) and note on IPv6 request process.
- Laura: short note on DNS egress patterns and webhook routing best practices.

If you can confirm whether certs will be customer-managed or managed by Redwood before the call, that will let Ben tailor the demo.

Hi Tom / Procurement team,

Thanks for sharing the RFI earlier this week. Attached is our initial control crosswalk that maps Meridian's questionnaire controls to Redwood controls, control owners, and proposed evidence artifacts. Highlights: 
- SOC 2 scope: hosted API, dedicated clusters, private deployment option; retention & logging controls included.
- Data residency: per-tenant routing and region pinning; we'll call out encryption and KMS posture per control.
- Kinds of artifacts: design docs, config snapshots, runbook excerpts, and short syslog samples.

title: Post-call: Candidate Scoring — SDK/Auth & eval next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Emma Nguyen <emma.nguyen@lexasearch.ai>, Derek Shaw <derek.shaw@lexasearch.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 15:23:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call: Candidate Scoring — SDK/Auth & eval next steps

Hi Emma + Derek,

Thanks again for a tight call earlier — appreciate the clarity on LexaSearch's reranking flow and the current constraints around PII and on-prem vector stores. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment before the tech deep-dive: 

Top-level recap
- Goal: plug Redwood's candidate-scoring layer into your existing retrieval pipeline to improve precision@k for query suggestions and zero-shot answers.
- Hard constraints: no raw customer PII leaves LexaSearch infra; embeddings currently hosted in your VPC vector DB (Pinecone-compatible API).
- Success metric: prioritized NDCG@10 and Precision@5 (we discussed also tracking MRR).

Technical asks we agreed on
1) Access: short-lived read-only credentials to a sanitized subset of your vector DB (or export of IDs + embeddings).
2) Auth model: determine whether we'll use mutual TLS or token-based (you indicated token via your gateway is likely).
3) SDK baseline: sample retrieval -> candidate scoring -> re-rank example in our SDK; confirm language (Node + Python).
4) Eval plan: initial 5k query set, holdout of 20% for offline NDCG sweeps, and a live A/B canary.

Attachments: candidate-scoring-integration-outline.pdf (high-level flow), initial-eval-prompts.csv (sample queries), auth-network-diagram.png (template for our ops).

Next steps (proposed)
- Emma/Derek: provide sanitized embedding access or export by EOD Sep 17. If token approach, share test token and gateway host.
- Vanessa/Connor (internal): prepare an SDK sample for Node and Python that demonstrates 1) auth handshake, 2) routing to the dedicated scorer endpoint, and 3) structured output JSON with top_k and score per candidate.
- Schedule: tech deep-dive next Wed 09/22 (45 min) to go over sample integration and run a small live inference test.

If anything above is off, flag it and we'll correct. Happy to iterate.

— Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0199

[attachment: candidate-scoring-integration-outline.pdf (pdf)]
[attachment: initial-eval-prompts.csv (csv)]

From: Derek Shaw <derek.shaw@lexasearch.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Emma Nguyen <emma.nguyen@lexasearch.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 17:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: Candidate Scoring — SDK/Auth & eval next steps

Ben — thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Embeddings: we can export a sanitized slice (IDs + 384d embeddings) as a gzipped CSV; no PII included. We'll deliver the export if using a batch offline eval. If you need runtime access to our VPC-hosted vector DB, token-based gateway is preferred (we don't open mTLS for third parties).
- Auth/token lifecycle: our gateway supports short-lived JWTs (1 hour) minted by our IAM. We can mint a long-lived test token scoped to read-only for the first integration sprint.
- Runtime routing: our infrastructure uses a regional edge (us-west) and we want to avoid cross-region token exchange — Redwood's canary should be deployed in us-west initially.

Questions for Redwood ops/engineers: 
1) Can the SDK sample demonstrate token refresh using a local Vault secret? We prefer not to embed tokens in CI.
2) For structured output, do you provide a schema for the candidate objects (id, score, provenance, retrieval_score)? We need to map fields into our downstream ranking aggregator.

We'll attach the sanitized export (pending internal approvals) and a short doc about the gateway constraints within 48 hours.

— Derek
Derek Shaw
Senior Search Engineer | LexaSearch
[attachment: lexasearch-embedding-sample.csv.gz (stub)]

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwood.com>
Cc: Emma Nguyen <emma.nguyen@lexasearch.ai>, Derek Shaw <derek.shaw@lexasearch.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2027 08:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: Candidate Scoring — SDK/Auth & eval next steps

Looping in Connor for the SDK/auth specifics and to propose an initial offline eval sweep. Connor — quick notes from the call: 
- LexaSearch will prefer token-based gateway and can provide a long-lived test token for us. They can also provide an embedding export if runtime access is too cumbersome.
- They want the structured candidate JSON to include: candidate_id, score (float), model_version, provenance (retrieval|scorer), and an optional explain token.
